userStories.title: Setup Prisma + schema inicial (Section, Category, Product)
userStories.description: As a developer, I want Prisma ORM configured with PostgreSQL and the initial schema for Section, Category, and Product, so that the application has a real persistence layer.
acceptanceCriteria: prisma and @prisma/client are added to dependencies
prisma/schema.prisma defines models Section, Category, Product with correct field types, relations, and constraints
prisma migrate dev creates the initial migration
prisma/seed.ts seeds initial data from lib/catalog-data.ts and lib/product-data.ts
DATABASE_URL is read from environment variable (never hardcoded)
src/lib/db.ts exports a singleton Prisma client
Typecheck passes
Lint passes
userStories.notes: DATABASE_URL is already set in compose.yaml and .env.example as postgresql://marketops:marketops@db:5432/marketops. Seed must match the IDs used in catalog-data.ts and product-data.ts to avoid breaking existing UI references.

userStories.title: Server actions para Secoes (criar, editar, ativar/desativar)
userStories.description: As a developer, I want Next.js server actions for sections CRUD, so that the sections-categories UI can persist section changes to the database.
acceptanceCriteria: src/lib/actions/sections.ts exports createSection, updateSection, toggleSectionStatus
Each action uses the Prisma client from src/lib/db.ts
Each action uses the 'use server' directive
Input is validated (name is required, non-empty) before writing
Each mutation calls revalidatePath('/secoes-categorias')
Typecheck passes
Lint passes
userStories.notes: Depends on US-001. Keep actions in src/lib/actions/ folder. Do not expose internal Prisma errors to the client.

userStories.title: Server actions para Categorias (criar, editar, ativar/desativar)
userStories.description: As a developer, I want Next.js server actions for categories CRUD, so that the sections-categories UI can persist category changes to the database.
acceptanceCriteria: src/lib/actions/categories.ts exports createCategory, updateCategory, toggleCategoryStatus
Each action uses the Prisma client from src/lib/db.ts
Each action uses the 'use server' directive
sectionId is validated against existing sections before insert or update
Each mutation calls revalidatePath('/secoes-categorias')
Typecheck passes
Lint passes

userStories.title: Conectar tela de Secoes e Categorias aos server actions
userStories.description: As an admin, I want the sections-categories screen to persist changes to the database, so that data is not lost on page refresh.
acceptanceCriteria: secoes-categorias/page.tsx becomes an async server component that fetches sections and categories from Prisma
SectionsCategoriesContent receives sections and categories as props instead of using initialSections/initialCategories from mock data
Form submissions call the server actions from US-002 and US-003
List updates after mutation without full client-side state management
Existing UI layout and visual design is preserved exactly
Typecheck passes
Lint passes
userStories.notes: Depends on US-002, US-003. Do not redesign the UI. The SectionsCategoriesContent component already has the correct visual structure.

userStories.title: Server actions para Produtos (criar, editar, ativar/desativar)
userStories.description: As a developer, I want Next.js server actions for products CRUD, so that the products UI can persist product changes to the database.
acceptanceCriteria: src/lib/actions/products.ts exports createProduct, updateProduct, toggleProductStatus
Each action uses the Prisma client from src/lib/db.ts
Each action uses the 'use server' directive
barcode and sku uniqueness is validated before insert
sectionId and categoryId are validated against existing records before write
Each mutation calls revalidatePath('/produtos')
Typecheck passes
Lint passes
userStories.notes: Depends on US-001. Product fields match the Product type in src/lib/product-data.ts.

userStories.title: Conectar tela de Produtos aos server actions
userStories.description: As an admin, I want the products screen to persist changes to the database, so that product data is not lost on page refresh.
acceptanceCriteria: produtos/page.tsx becomes an async server component that fetches products, sections, and categories from Prisma
ProductsContent receives data as props instead of using initialProducts from mock data
Form submissions call the server actions from US-005
List updates after mutation
Existing UI layout and visual design is preserved exactly
Typecheck passes
Lint passes
userStories.notes: Depends on US-005. Do not redesign the UI.

userStories.title: Schema de Estoque + server actions (leitura e ajuste)
userStories.description: As a developer, I want a StockEntry model in Prisma and server actions for reading and adjusting stock levels, so that the stock screen shows real data.
acceptanceCriteria: prisma/schema.prisma adds StockEntry model with fields: id, productId (FK), quantity, type (entrada | saida | ajuste), createdAt
src/lib/actions/stock.ts exports getStockSummary and adjustStock
getStockSummary returns aggregated stock per product (sum of all entries)
adjustStock validates that productId exists before writing
Migration file is updated via prisma migrate dev
Typecheck passes
Lint passes
userStories.notes: Depends on US-001. Stock level = sum of StockEntry.quantity per product (positive = entrada, negative = saida).

userStories.title: Conectar tela de Estoque aos server actions
userStories.description: As an admin, I want the stock screen to show real stock levels from the database, so that inventory reflects actual data.
acceptanceCriteria: estoque/page.tsx becomes an async server component that fetches stock summary from Prisma
StockContent receives real data as props instead of using mock stock-data.ts
Low-stock alert is based on product.minimumStock compared to real aggregated stock
Existing UI layout and visual design is preserved exactly
Typecheck passes
Lint passes
userStories.notes: Depends on US-007. Do not redesign the UI.

userStories.title: Schema de Caixas + server actions (abrir, fechar, visualizar)
userStories.description: As a developer, I want a CashRegister model in Prisma and server actions for managing cash registers, so that the caixas screen shows real data.
acceptanceCriteria: prisma/schema.prisma adds CashRegister model with fields: id, name, status (open | closed), openedAt, closedAt, openingBalance, closingBalance
src/lib/actions/cash-registers.ts exports getCashRegisters, openCashRegister, closeCashRegister
openCashRegister validates that the register is not already open before writing
closeCashRegister validates that the register is currently open before writing
Each mutation calls revalidatePath('/caixas')
Migration file is updated via prisma migrate dev
Typecheck passes
Lint passes

userStories.title: Conectar tela de Caixas aos server actions
userStories.description: As an admin, I want the cash registers screen to show real data from the database and allow opening and closing registers.
acceptanceCriteria: caixas/page.tsx becomes an async server component that fetches cash registers from Prisma
CashRegistersContent receives real data as props instead of using mock cash-data.ts
Open and close actions persist to the database and revalidate the page
Existing UI layout and visual design is preserved exactly
Typecheck passes
Lint passes
userStories.notes: Depends on US-009. Do not redesign the UI.

userStories.title: Setup NextAuth com credentials provider + model User no Prisma
userStories.description: As an admin, I want to log in to the system with email and password, so that only authorized users can access the admin panel.
acceptanceCriteria: next-auth and @auth/prisma-adapter are added to dependencies
bcryptjs is added to dependencies for password hashing
prisma/schema.prisma adds User model with fields: id, email (unique), passwordHash, role (admin | operator), createdAt
src/lib/auth.ts configures NextAuth with CredentialsProvider, JWT strategy, and PrismaAdapter
Login page at /login with email and password fields, matching existing visual style (slate-950 background, emerald accent)
Passwords are hashed with bcryptjs before storage (never stored in plaintext)
NEXTAUTH_SECRET is read from environment variable (must be set in .env.example)
prisma/seed.ts creates one default admin user (email: admin@marketops.local, password: admin123)
Migration file is updated via prisma migrate dev
Typecheck passes
Lint passes
userStories.notes: Depends on US-001. Critical: never store plaintext passwords. NEXTAUTH_SECRET must be at least 32 characters. Add NEXTAUTH_SECRET and NEXTAUTH_URL to .env.example.

userStories.title: Middleware de protecao de rotas autenticadas
userStories.description: As an admin, I want all admin routes to require authentication, so that unauthenticated users are redirected to the login page.
acceptanceCriteria: src/middleware.ts protects all routes except /login and /api/auth/*
Unauthenticated requests to protected routes are redirected to /login
Authenticated requests to /login are redirected to /
Static assets (/_next/*, /favicon.ico) are not blocked by middleware
Middleware matcher is explicitly configured to avoid performance issues
Typecheck passes
Lint passes
userStories.notes: Depends on US-011. Use the withAuth helper from next-auth/middleware or implement manual token check via getToken.

userStories.title: Atualizar sidebar para MVP 2 com usuario logado
userStories.description: As an admin, I want the sidebar to show my logged-in name and reflect that the system is now on MVP 2, so that the interface communicates the current state.
acceptanceCriteria: Sidebar bottom section shows logged-in user name and role from session
MVP label in sidebar bottom is updated from 'MVP 1' to 'MVP 2'
Configuracoes item remains disabled with tooltip 'Modulo planejado para as proximas etapas'
All other nav items remain functional as before
Logout button or link is available in the sidebar
Typecheck passes
Lint passes
userStories.notes: Depends on US-011. AppSidebar is a client component ('use client') so useSession() from next-auth/react can be used directly.
